claim: Dagger uses container technology to isolate agent environments, preventing cross-agent interference when multiple agents work in parallel.
actionable: Implement container-based isolation for your agentic workflows using Dagger to ensure agents run in completely isolated environments.
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Containing Agent Chaos — Solomon Hykes

claim: Agent environments must support portability, isolation, and observability to avoid lock-in to specific models, cloud providers, or IDEs.
actionable: Design agent infrastructure with portability and IDE-agnostic interfaces; avoid solutions that create vendor lock-in to specific IDEs or cloud providers.
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Containing Agent Chaos — Solomon Hykes

claim: Agentic development is fragmenting into vertical end-to-end monoliths where platforms (IDEs, model providers) add proprietary ways to customize agent environments rather than supporting standards.
actionable: Resist proprietary solutions; advocate for and build with open standards for agent environments to prevent vendor fragmentation.
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Containing Agent Chaos — Solomon Hykes

claim: Local execution support is a critical requirement for standardizing agent development environments; cloud-only solutions will not be ubiquitous.
actionable: Ensure any agent environment solution supports local execution as a first-class use case, not an afterthought.
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Containing Agent Chaos — Solomon Hykes

claim: Docker and Docker Compose were designed as prototypes and are not agent-native; new UX design is needed specifically for agentic workflows.
actionable: Don't force agents to work with Docker Compose's original human-focused syntax; design new configuration languages optimized for agent workflows.
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Containing Agent Chaos — Solomon Hykes

claim: Container technology combined with Git and standardized APIs (OpenAI spec, MCP) provides a sufficient standards foundation for building agent environments.
actionable: Leverage existing standards (containers, Git, MCP) when building agent infrastructure rather than inventing entirely new primitives.
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Containing Agent Chaos — Solomon Hykes

claim: Developers are transitioning from individual contributors to platform engineers who must manage and enable multiple AI agents, similar to how we manage teams.
actionable: Train developers in platform engineering skills to manage teams of AI agents rather than just writing code themselves.
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Containing Agent Chaos — Solomon Hykes

claim: Workflow orchestration and CI/CD infrastructure are converging with agentic systems, with CI/CD becoming runtime infrastructure for agent workflows.
actionable: Adopt workflow orchestration platforms that support both traditional CI/CD and long-running agent tasks interchangeably.
source_title: [AIEWF Preview] Containing Agent Chaos — Solomon Hykes
